projectStatus.title: Project Status
projectStatus.description: Control whether the project is live or paused

cache.title: Cache
cache.description: Clear cached build artifacts and dependency installs

transfer.title: Transfer & Clone
transfer.description: Move or duplicate this project to a different environment

danger.title: Danger Zone
danger.body: Deleting this project will permanently remove all deployments, custom domains, environment variables, and analytics data.

routeRules.title: Route rules
routeRules.description: Rate-limit, ban, and allow/deny rules enforced at the edge — applied instantly, no redeploy.
routeRules.empty: No route rules yet. Add one below.

live.title: Live backup
live.description: Real-time progress for the run you just kicked off. Survives page refresh.

destinations.title: Backup destinations
destinations.description: Per-user storage targets. Add S3-compatible buckets, SFTP servers, or local disks.

services.title: Services + policies
services.description: Each service can have its own backup policy (schedule + destination). Project-level defaults land in Chunk 2.
services.empty: This project has no services yet. Service-level backups appear here once you add some.
services.createPolicyHint: Create a backup policy for this service

recent.title: Recent backups
recent.description: The 25 most recent runs across all services in this project.

webmail.title: Managed by openship
webmail.description: Webmail uses a fixed build and start pipeline — install, build, and run commands are not configurable. Redeploy from the mail overview to pick up upstream changes.

services.title: Per-service settings
services.descriptionTemplate: This project has {serviceLabel}. Build commands, framework, ports, and run commands live on each service row — view and edit them in the Services tab.

runtime.title: Runtime configuration
runtime.description: Read-only. Edit through the deploy wizard so each change ships as a new version.

env.title: Environment variables
env.description: Encrypted at rest. Secrets stay masked — only the values you change are written.

envVars.applyText: Saved. Apply these to the running service — it restarts with the new env, no clone or rebuild.
envVars.title: Environment variables
envVars.description: Production. Encrypted at rest. Secrets show as set — type to replace; leave blank to keep.

toast.applying: Restarting the service with the new env — no rebuild.
toast.applyFailed: Couldn't apply the env to the running service

gitInfo.chooseBranch: Choose which branch to deploy from your repository

connectFirst.title: Connect GitHub first
connectFirst.description: Link your GitHub account to connect a repository to this project.

link.title: Link a Repository
link.description: Connect a GitHub repository to enable webhook-triggered deployments.

picker.title: Select a repository
picker.description: Choose a repository to link to this project

appBanner.title: GitHub App not installed
appBanner.description: Install the Openship GitHub App on your account or organization to enable auto-deploy and webhook-triggered deployments for cloud projects.

webhookBanner.title: Enable auto-deploy
webhookBanner.description: Auto-deploy needs a direct webhook endpoint. Expose this Openship API on a public URL or configure a verified webhook domain for direct delivery.

source.title: Source Repository
source.description: Git integration, deployment triggers, and repository health

webhookEndpoint.title: Webhook Endpoint
webhookEndpoint.descriptionSet: GitHub delivers push events directly to this domain
webhookEndpoint.descriptionUnset: Choose a domain for direct webhook delivery

webhookCard.title: Webhook
webhookCard.descInactive: Webhook has not been configured or is not responding

rollbackStrategy.title: Rollback strategy
rollbackStrategy.descRebuild: Rollback re-clones & rebuilds the old commit — cheaper, but slower and can fail if that commit no longer builds.
rollbackStrategy.descInstant: Keeps each version's build artifact on disk for rollback in seconds. Uses extra disk, capped by the history limit.

rollbackHistory.title: Rollback history
rollbackHistory.descSnapshot: How many recent versions keep their build artifact for rollback. Older ones are pruned to reclaim disk.
rollbackHistory.descGit: Applies only to Instant (snapshot) mode — Git mode keeps no artifacts to retain.

commits.title: Recent Commits
commits.subtitle: Latest commits on {branch}
commits.emptyDesc: Push to your repository and recent commit activity will appear here.

cloneToken.title: Clone Token Override
cloneToken.description: Project-scoped token used by the clone module before falling back to your global token or the GitHub App
cloneToken.explainer: Paste a GitHub PAT here to use ONLY for this project. Highest priority in the clone chain - overrides your settings-level token and the GitHub App.

toast.rollbackWindowOne: Keeping the last {count} version for rollback
toast.rollbackWindowOther: Keeping the last {count} versions for rollback
toast.appNotInstalled: GitHub App is not installed for this account

logs.noRuntimeDesc: This project is deployed as a static app, so there is no running server process to stream logs from.
logs.targetDescProject: Switch between the project runtime and service runtimes.
logs.selectService: Select a service to view its runtime logs.

resources.title: Machine Power
resources.description: Configure the machine power for your project.

toast.cpuRange: CPU must be between 0.25 and 4.00 cores
toast.memoryRange: Memory must be between 128 MB and 8192 MB

sleep.title: Machine Mode
sleep.infoBefore: When there are requests, your container stays running. After inactivity, it sleeps to save costs. On the next request, it wakes up

deleteDialog.title: Delete Project
deleteDialog.bodyPrefix: Are you sure you want to delete
deleteDialog.bodySuffix: ? This action cannot be undone and will permanently delete all project data, deployments, and settings.

deletion.title: Delete project
deletion.deleteAllOn: Removes the project app and every branch environment under it.
deletion.deleteAllOff: Removes only this environment. Other branches stay.
deletion.wipeOn: Volumes will be permanently removed - database contents, caches, uploads, everything stored by these services will be gone.
deletion.wipeOff: Off: containers are removed but their named volumes stay on disk. Re-attach them later by name.
deletion.amberWipe: All deployments, domains, env vars, and persistent service data will be permanently removed.
deletion.amberNoWipe: All deployments, domains, env vars, and analytics will be permanently removed.

routing.title: Routing
routing.description: Rewrites, redirects & headers. Applied on save — no rebuild.

toast.title: Routing
toast.updated: Routing updated — applied to the live deployment.

add.title: Add domain
add.description: Add a route: pick a free or custom domain and the port it maps to.
add.servesPathHint: Requests to this domain are served from this path in your build output.
add.servesPathRedeploy: New paths take effect on your next deploy.
add.externalIngressDesc: TLS terminates upstream (Cloudflare Tunnel, load balancer). Verify by TXT only — the domain need not point at this server, and we won't issue a certificate.
add.noWww: Enter the domain without "www." — turn on "Include www" below to add it.

dns.title: DNS Records
dns.descPreview: Add these records at your DNS provider, then click Add domain to attach it
dns.descApply: Apply these records at your DNS provider, then wait for propagation
dns.infoPreview: Add these records first — they don't change after adding, so propagation starts now. Then press Add domain to attach it, and finally Verify once DNS resolves.
dns.infoApply: DNS changes can take up to 48 hours to propagate globally. Once the records resolve, click Verify below — we'll check DNS, mark the domain active, and provision SSL in the background.

records.hint: Add these at your DNS provider, then click Verify.
records.none: No records to add for this domain.

verifyMissing.both: Neither the CNAME nor the TXT record is resolving yet — add the records below, wait for DNS to propagate, then Verify.
verifyMissing.cname: The CNAME record isn't resolving yet — add it below, wait for DNS to propagate, then Verify.
verifyMissing.txt: The TXT verification record isn't resolving yet — add it below, wait for DNS to propagate, then Verify.

portHint.body: Nothing responded on port {port} — requests may 502 until the app listens there.
portHint.bodyService: Service "{service}": nothing responded on port {port} — requests may 502 until it listens there.

outputHint.body: No build output found at {path} — check your Output Directory and redeploy.

edit.title: Edit domains
edit.descServer: Edit which internal port each domain should route to.
edit.descStatic: Edit which static path each domain should serve.

addRoute.emptySuffix: to point a domain at a service's port.

editRoute.title: Edit route
editRoute.disabledWarning: Service is disabled - routes are inactive until the service is re-enabled.

certUpload.title: Upload certificate
certUpload.desc: Serve TLS from your own certificate (bring-your-own or Cloudflare Origin CA) instead of Let's Encrypt. Use this when an upstream proxy fronts this domain and the origin still needs a real cert (Cloudflare Full-strict). Certbot is disabled for this domain once a certificate is uploaded — renew by uploading a new one.

toast.enterPort: Enter the port this domain should route to (1–65535).
toast.addedCustom: {label} added — apply the DNS records, then Verify.
toast.verifiedSuccess: {hostname} verified — SSL is provisioning in the background.
toast.sslNoCert: No valid certificate found for {hostname} yet. If you just deployed, give Let's Encrypt a moment, then recheck.
toast.completeEndpoints: Complete every domain and mapped port before saving
toast.enterPortShort: Enter the port this domain should route to.
toast.noServicePort: No service publishes port {port}. Add that port to a service first.
